text: The surgeon evaluates the gastric pouch, Peterson's defect, and JJ, confirming no internal hernias. The common channel is examined from the JJ to the terminal ileum, showing no issues. Laxity is observed in the common channel, with contrast visible moving retrograde into the JJ and up to the biliopancreatic limb.

The surgeon performs a plication of the common channel to the biliopancreatic limb at the site of observed laxity, using two permanent sutures to align the tissues and prevent kinks, aiming to prevent further intussusception and alleviate symptoms.
